headline: Iran's speaker says US negotiations have progressed but far from final deal
description: Iran said it had been 'victorious in the field' during weeks of war and had only agreed to a temporary truce with the US because its demands had been met, Iranian Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said on Saturday. The two-week ceasefire is set to end on Wednesday unless it is renewed.

headline: Obsession with primaries takes hold in French politics a year out from presidential race
Terrorism investigation opened after at least six killed in Kyiv shooting
In southern Lebanon, displaced people return to their bombed villages
description: As the ceasefire takes effect, residents of southern Lebanon, Hezbollah's stronghold, return to their villages to witness the destruction caused by Israeli airstrikes.

headline: Louis Verret paints the drama of football in watercolor
description: The artist based in Aubervilliers, a suburb of Paris, paints football's masters as if they are freeze-frames, channeling pure emotion straight to the goal.

headline: Why it's important to teach children to recognize birdsong
description: Knowing which bird is chirping or warbling is actually child's play, as long as you approach it methodically. We asked experts to guide us in this learning process. Along the way, they gave some compelling reasons to distinguish the blackbird's song from the chaffinch's tune.

headline: A photographic journey into the daily lives of home care aides
description: Between autumn 2024 and summer 2025, photographer Vincent Gouriou followed several home health aides in Dordogne, southwest France, documenting their often difficult daily routines as they traveled to isolated homes, provided care and offered a listening ear.

Ireland, striving to become Europe's data center hub, falls victim to its own success
description: Despite major pressure on the power grid and the risk of rising greenhouse gas emissions, Dublin wants to keep building these facilities for tech giants and establish itself as a European leader in the sector.

headline: The pitfalls of Hungary's post-Orban transition
description: Riding a powerful wave of opposition to the outgoing government, Hungary's new prime minister, Péter Magyar, now has to balance the expectations of both left- and right-wing voters while clarifying his positions, especially regarding Ukraine.

headline: How the tech lobby made secrecy part of EU law on data centers
description: Microsoft and the tech industry lobby secured a provision from the EU to keep environmental data about the massive data centers they operate in Europe confidential, according to an investigation by the Investigate Europe consortium, in collaboration with Le Monde.
byline: Ella Joyner,  Léa Prati and  Nico Schmidt
